title: Campaign Monitor vs Flodesk (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Campaign Monitor and Flodesk email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which is best for your business.
h1: Campaign Monitor vs Flodesk: Detailed Comparison
intro: Campaign Monitor and Flodesk are both powerful email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences and use cases. Campaign Monitor excels at multi-brand management for agencies, while Flodesk prioritizes visual design and simplicity for creators and small businesses. This comparison will help you determine which tool aligns with your email marketing needs.
choose_a_if: You manage multiple client brands or are an agency needing centralized control
You want the lowest entry price and scale pricing with subscriber count
You need advanced segmentation and transactional email capabilities
choose_b_if: You're a visual brand, photographer, designer, or creator prioritizing beautiful aesthetics
You want unlimited subscribers with predictable flat-rate pricing
You need built-in checkout forms and link-in-bio features for ecommerce or digital products

faq.q: Is Campaign Monitor better than Flodesk?
faq.a: Neither is universally better—it depends on your needs. Campaign Monitor excels for agencies and multi-brand management with lower entry pricing, while Flodesk wins for visual creators and small businesses wanting unlimited subscribers and beautiful design-focused templates. Check the customer ratings: Flodesk has 4.8 on G2 vs Campaign Monitor's 4.1.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Campaign Monitor or Flodesk?
faq.a: Campaign Monitor starts at just $11/month, making it cheaper at entry level. However, Flodesk offers flat-rate pricing at $38/month with unlimited subscribers, which becomes better value as your list grows. If you have over 5,000 subscribers, Flodesk's unlimited model typically costs less overall.

faq.q: Can I switch from Flodesk to Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms allow you to export your subscriber lists and email campaigns. Most email marketing tools support standard CSV exports and API integrations, making migration possible though it requires some technical setup. Plan the transition during a natural campaign break to minimize disruption.
